evals.name: dry_run_april_2026
evals.prompt: Run a dry-run cleanup of all MEO SharePoint klantenmappen. I want to see what files would be deleted, how much space we'd free, and a detailed breakdown by cleanup rule. Don't actually delete anything yet - just generate the report.
evals.expected_output: Excel report with tabs: Summary (files analyzed, space savings estimate), Cleanup Rules (breakdown per rule), File Details (list of files that would be deleted), Client Status (which clients are >2 years inactive), Protected Folders (confirmation that materialenmappen and standards are safe)

evals.name: execute_approved_cleanup
evals.prompt: Based on the dry-run report from April 2026, execute the approved cleanup. Delete all files that were flagged in R1 (old PDFs), R2 (old PSDs), R6 (large old files), R7 (log files), and R8 (system files). Don't touch R3, R4, R5 yet - those need separate approval. Generate final report with 'EXECUTED' status and email summary to steven@wijzijnmeo.nl.
evals.expected_output: Execution report showing: files actually deleted, space freed (in GB), confirmation emails sent, no errors in protected folders, detailed audit trail of what was removed

evals.name: client_folder_protection_test
evals.prompt: Test that the skill correctly protects critical folders. Scan 'Wij zijn MEO - Klanten - A' folder and verify that: (1) any 'materialenmappen' subfolders are never included in deletion list, (2) '0 Standaard mappen & documenten' is completely protected, (3) R5 (inactive clients rule) would skip this folder if it had materialenmappen, but can still delete entire folder if it's truly inactive and has no materialenmappen.
evals.expected_output: Verification report confirming: materialenmappen protection is active, standard folders are protected, R5 exception logic is working correctly, dry-run shows 0 files would be deleted from protected hierarchies
